LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ASIF AIVAZOV, SABINA PORTYANSKY, AND STAS PORTYANKSY AMENDED NOTICE OF NOTICE FOR REHEARING OF DEFENDANTS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1008 IS DENIED, TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION EXCEPT FOR THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION; THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION SURVIVES THE MOTION TO STRIKE. MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 657 IS MOOT. OBJECTION TO LATE FILED OPPOSITION OVERRULED, PLAINTIFFS RESPONDED ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANTS' ADDITIONAL OBJECTIONS SUSTAINED AS TO PORTYANSKY DECLARATION OBJECTION #1 ONLY, REMAINING OBJECTIONS OVERRULED. (PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL WILL PREPARE THE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ASIF AIVAZOV, A MINOR, SABINA PORTYANSKY, AND, STAS PORTYANSKY MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND ORDER THEREON. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THIS MOTION IS TREATED AS A MOTION TO VACATE. THE COURT'S 11/17/2009 ORDER AWARDING FEES IS VACATED, UNDER THE MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY PROVISION OF CCP SECTION 473(B). THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR FEES. THE COURT FINDS THE LODESTAR AMOUNT FOR FEES AND COSTS PREVIOUSLY AWARDED WAS PROPERLY CALCULATED. HOWEVER, IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION, THE COURT REDUCES THAT AMOUNT BY $20,000.00 DUE TO CIRCUMSTANCES RENDERING THE AWARD UNJUST: THE DISABILITY OF ONE PLAINTIFF AND THE LACK OF PLAINTIFFS' INCOME. THE COURT AWARDS DEFENDANTS ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $22,282.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Progress Report Re; Receipt & Acknowledgment Of Deposit Of Money Into A Blocked Account.
Off Calendar Dec-28-2010 Continued To Jan-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Nov-18-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. Everett A. Hewlett, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Scott Hughes, #12365.

J. Michael Phelps, Esq. appeared with petitioner Sabrina Portyansky, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor Asif Aivazov, who was not present at today's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and examined.
Stas Poryansky was placed under oath and served as Russian speaking interpreter for petitioner.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $3,333.00.
Attorney's Fees - $2,500.00.
Medical Expenses - $0.00.
Costs - $0.00.

The net balance of $833.00 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, Foothill Farms Branch, Sacramento, CA. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders approving the compromise of a disputed claim of a minor, and to deposit money into a blocked account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is continued to December 28, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. for progress reported re: Receipt & Acknowledgment of Deposit of Money Into a Blocked Account.

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion For Attorney Fees And Order Thereon;
Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS ASIF AIVAZOV, A MINOR, SABINA PORTYANSKY, and, STAS PORTYANSKY Motion for Reconsideration of Motion for Attorney Fees and Order Thereon. Appearances: J. Michael Phelps, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 433-3733, appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Michael K. Tcheng, Esq. of Colombatto Sims LLP (415) 391-6182, appearing for Defendants and opposing party; Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Beckman Marquez Dowling LLP (415) 495-8500, appearing for Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: This motion is treated as a motion to vacate. The Court's 11/17/2009 order awarding fees is vacated, under the mandatory and discretionary provision of CCP Section 473(b). The Court grants defendants' request for fees. The court finds the lodestar amount for fees and costs previously awarded was properly calculated. However, in light of plaintiffs' opposition, the Court reduces that amount by $20,000.00 due to circumstances rendering the award unjust: the disability of one plaintiff and the lack of plaintiffs' income. The Court awards defendants attorney fees and costs in the amount of $22,282.00. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Amended Notice Of Notice For Rehearing Of Defts' Special Motion To Strike Complaint For Damages And, In The Alternative, Fora New Trial; Supplemental
Off Calendar Jun-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS ASIF AIVAZOV, SABINA PORTYANSKY, AND STAS PORTYANKSY Amended Notice of Notice for Rehearing Of Defendants' Special Motion To Strike Complaint For Damages and, In The Alternative, Motion for a New Trial. Appearances: Steven Rosenthal, Esq. & Steven Ruth, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven Rosenthal, (415) 928-7300, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Beckman Marquez Dowling LLP (415) 495-8500, appearing for Defendants Scott & Joyce Cox, and opposing party; Michael K. Tcheng, of Colombatto Sims LLP (415) 391-6182, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendants Scott & Joyce Cox, and opposing party. Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for reconsideration pursuant to CCP section 1008 is denied, to all causes of action except for the sixth cause of action; the sixth cause of action survives the motion to strike. Motion for new trial pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 657 is moot. Objection to late filed opposition overruled, plaintiffs responded on the merits. Defendants' additional objections sustained as to Portyansky declaration objection #1 only, remaining objections overruled. (Plaintiffs' counsel will prepare the Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Apr-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOYCE COX, and, SCOTT COX'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES. Appearances: Steven S. Rosenthal, Esq. Law Office of Steven S. Rosenthal, (415) 928-7300, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Beckman Marquez Dowling LLP (415) 495-8500, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Michael K. Tchen, Esq. of Colombatto Sims LLP (415) 391-6182, also appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court rules as follows: Granted without leave to amend. No authority to grant leave to amend. Also, Evidentiary objections to Portyansky Declaration 1 & 2 are sustained, 3 is overruled; Evidentiary objections to Stroud Declaration 1, & 3, are overruled, 2 is sustained.
